examples.sentence: During the meeting, I realized that Greg was a real time-waster, constantly sidetracking the discussion with irrelevant points.
examples.translation: During the meeting, I realized that Greg was a real time-waster, constantly sidetracking the discussion with irrelevant points.

examples.sentence: You shouldn't hire someone who's just going to be a time-waster; efficiency is key in this project.
examples.translation: You shouldn't hire someone who's just going to be a time-waster; efficiency is key in this project.

examples.sentence: She was labeled as a time-waster because she often delayed the team's progress without contributing any useful ideas.
examples.translation: She was labeled as a time-waster because she often delayed the team's progress without contributing any useful ideas.

examples.sentence: Scrolling endlessly through social media can be a serious time-waster when you have deadlines to meet.

examples.sentence: Playing that repetitive game on your phone is just a time-waster that won’t help you learn anything new.

examples.sentence: If you think watching back-to-back reality TV shows is productive, you might be underestimating how much of a time-waster it really is.

examples.sentence: The old software proved to be a time-waster, slowing down the team’s workflow without adding any value.

examples.sentence: She felt like a time-waster during the project because her tasks were constantly delayed and ineffective.

examples.sentence: They realized that the frequent meetings had become a time-waster, consuming hours without meaningful outcomes.

examples.sentence: If the training sessions continue like this, they're just going to be a time-waster for everyone involved.

examples.sentence: We don’t know if their new strategy will succeed, but time will tell whether it pays off in the long run.

examples.sentence: Only time will tell if the medication will have the desired effect on the patient’s recovery.
examples.translation: Only time will tell if the medication will have the desired effect on the patient’s recovery.

examples.sentence: I’m not sure how the market will react to the change, but time will tell what the consequences are.

examples.sentence: The success of their partnership is uncertain for now; time will tell how well they work together.
examples.translation: The success of their partnership is uncertain for now; time will tell how well they work together.

examples.sentence: We can’t judge the project's impact just yet—time will tell what the final results look like.

examples.sentence: They decided not to rush their decision since time will tell which candidate is best suited for the job.

examples.sentence: When asked if the changes would improve efficiency, the CEO simply replied, 'time will tell.'

examples.sentence: Tom acted like a tin god at the office, behaving as if his small role made him the ultimate authority.
examples.translation: Tom acted like a tin god at the office, behaving as if his small role made him the ultimate authority.

examples.sentence: She was considered a bit of a tin god in the local club, boasting loudly about her minor achievements.

examples.sentence: No one took the new manager seriously because he carried himself like a tin god despite his limited experience.

examples.sentence: If you keep acting like a tin god, people will start mocking you instead of respecting you.
examples.translation: If you keep acting like a tin god, people will start mocking you instead of respecting you.

examples.sentence: The statue in the town square was a tin god to some, though it held no real significance to the community.

examples.sentence: They treated the outdated policy like a tin god, revering it despite how irrelevant it had become.
examples.translation: They treated the outdated policy like a tin god, revering it despite how irrelevant it had become.

examples.sentence: The leader was a tin god in his own mind, but most people saw through the superficial authority he claimed.

examples.sentence: The department head was seen as a tin god by his small team, who gave him undue admiration despite his limited power.

examples.sentence: Even though he was just a minor official, people treated him like a tin god, hanging on his every word.

examples.sentence: The community elevated their local coach to the level of a tin god, praising him excessively for small victories.

examples.sentence: The legendary Tinker to Evers to Chance trio was celebrated for their flawless double-play coordination during the early 1900s.

examples.sentence: Baseball historians often reference Tinker to Evers to Chance as the epitome of defensive teamwork on the field.

examples.sentence: When the Cubs’ infield was performing well, fans would chant the names Tinker to Evers to Chance in admiration.

examples.sentence: The coordinated plays by Tinker to Evers to Chance helped secure many victories for Chicago during their heyday.

examples.sentence: The marketing team acted like a Tinker to Evers to Chance, methodically blocking any new ideas from competing agencies.

examples.sentence: Whenever we try to push changes, those three always act as a Tinker to Evers to Chance, frustrating our efforts together.

examples.sentence: You can count on that group to be a Tinker to Evers to Chance, working in perfect sync to prevent any progress.

examples.sentence: The rivalry between departments often felt like a Tinker to Evers to Chance scenario, with each side predictably blocking the other.
